title: QA: get_matcher_context exposes full user prompt to regex matchers (ReDoS surface)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/hooks/mod.rs:485-495
## Violation
`get_matcher_context` falls back to the user prompt as the matcher
target for non-tool events:
```rust
fn get_matcher_context(input: &HookInput) -> String {
    if let Some(tool_name) = &input.tool_name {
        return tool_name.clone();
    }
    if let Some(prompt) = &input.prompt {
        return prompt.clone();
    }
    input.event.config_key().to_string()
}
```
For a `UserPromptSubmit` event, this means **every configured hook
matcher regex is tested against the full user prompt**. The MAX_REGEX_SIZE
limit prevents catastrophic regex compilation, but it does not bound
regex *match* time on a 200 KB prompt against a partially-backtracking
pattern. There is no timeout on `re.is_match(context)` (line 532).
A malicious user prompt of pathological length combined with any
hand-written matcher regex creates a ReDoS vector that blocks the hook
dispatch path.
## Mandated refactor
Truncate `context` to a configurable limit (e.g. 8 KB) before matching,
and explicitly hash-prefix long contexts so matchers cannot rely on
arbitrary-length input. Or use a regex implementation with bounded
match time (the `regex` crate is linear-time only for non-backtracking
patterns — confirm and document that). Add `debug!` log when truncation
fires. Consider running `is_match` in a `tokio::time::timeout` wrapper.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
